description: Magnetic Resonance Imaging Technologists operate Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scanners. During scans, they monitor patient safety and comfort, and view images of the areas being scanned to ensure image quality. They may administer gadolinium contrast dosages intravenously, interview patients, explain MRI procedures, and position patients on the examining table. description: Aircraft Launch and Recovery Officers plan and direct the operation and maintenance of catapults, arresting gear, and associated mechanical, hydraulic, and control systems involved  in aircraft carrier takeoff and landing operations. description: Special Forces Officers lead elite teams that implement unconventional operations by air, land, or sea during combat or peacetime. description: Artillery & Missile Crew Members target, fire, and maintain weapons used to destroy enemy positions, aircraft, and vessels. Field artillery crew typically use guns, cannons, and howitzers in ground combat operations, while air defense artillery crew members predominantly use missiles and rockets. Naval artillery crew members predominantly use torpedoes and missiles launched from a ship or submarine. Their duties include testing, inspecting, and storing ammunition, missiles, and torpedoes; conducting preventive and routine maintenance on weapons and related equipment; establishing and maintaining radio and wire communications; and operating weapons targeting, firing, and launch computer systems.
